syntactical_description: Fails to conduct current through the power contacts
reasoning_trace: If the power contacts fail to conduct, the motor cannot be energized at all. This is a complete loss of the primary function.
end_effect: Complete inability to power on, initialize, or accept user inputs.

syntactical_description: Conducts current at insufficient capacity due to degraded contact surface
reasoning_trace: Degraded contact surfaces increase resistance, leading to voltage drop and reduced motor performance. The motor runs but at reduced capacity.
end_effect: Primary function operates, but at a noticeably degraded speed, range, or capacity.

syntactical_description: Intermittently conducts current through the power contacts
reasoning_trace: Intermittent conduction causes the motor to cycle on/off unexpectedly. This is an abrupt cessation of the primary function during operation.
end_effect: Abrupt and unrecoverable cessation of the primary function during operation.

syntactical_description: Uncommanded conducting of current through power contacts
reasoning_trace: Welded or stuck contacts energize the motor without a command. This is unintended activation of moving parts, a safety hazard.
end_effect: Unintended activation or inability to stop hazardous moving parts.

syntactical_description: Conducts current too late after coil energization command
reasoning_trace: Delayed contact engagement means the motor starts late. Functionally degraded but not a total loss.
end_effect: Primary function operates, but at a noticeably degraded speed, range, or capacity.

syntactical_description: Fails to cease conducting current when coil is de-energized
reasoning_trace: If contacts don't open, the motor cannot be stopped. This is a direct safety hazard — inability to stop moving parts.
end_effect: Unintended activation or inability to stop hazardous moving parts.

syntactical_description: Erratically conducts current with unstable contact resistance
reasoning_trace: Erratic conduction causes voltage fluctuations and unstable motor output. Quality of operation is degraded.
end_effect: Product output is erratic, unstable, or of poor quality compared to specifications.

syntactical_description: Fails to generate magnetic force to close the contactor
reasoning_trace: Without magnetic force, the contactor cannot close. The motor cannot be started. Total loss.
end_effect: Complete inability to power on, initialize, or accept user inputs.

syntactical_description: Generates insufficient magnetic force to fully seat the armature
reasoning_trace: Insufficient force means contacts may not fully close, causing high resistance and degraded performance.
end_effect: Primary function operates, but at a noticeably degraded speed, range, or capacity.

syntactical_description: Intermittently generates magnetic force due to coil connection fault
reasoning_trace: Intermittent coil activation means the motor cuts in and out. Abrupt cessation of primary function.
end_effect: Abrupt and unrecoverable cessation of the primary function during operation.

syntactical_description: Uncommanded coil generates magnetic force without control signal
reasoning_trace: Spurious coil energization closes the contactor and starts the motor without command. Unintended moving parts.
end_effect: Unintended activation or inability to stop hazardous moving parts.

syntactical_description: Generates magnetic force too late after voltage is applied to coil
reasoning_trace: Delayed response means slower motor start. A degraded but functional condition.
end_effect: Primary function operates, but at a noticeably degraded speed, range, or capacity.

syntactical_description: Fails to cease generate magnetic force when supply is removed
reasoning_trace: If the coil remains energized, the contactor stays closed and the motor cannot be stopped. Safety hazard.
end_effect: Unintended activation or inability to stop hazardous moving parts.

syntactical_description: Erratically generates magnetic force causing armature chatter
reasoning_trace: Chattering armature creates noise and vibration, and degrades contact life. Nuisance condition.
end_effect: Nuisance user experience due to non-hazardous heat, vibration, or elevated noise levels.

syntactical_description: Fails to signal contact status to the control circuit
reasoning_trace: No status signal means the control system cannot confirm contactor state. Loss of interface capability.
end_effect: Total inability to interface, physically or digitally, with required external systems or accessories.

syntactical_description: Signals contact status with degraded accuracy or insufficient current capacity
reasoning_trace: Signal is present but unreliable. Erratic/poor quality output from this auxiliary function.
end_effect: Product output is erratic, unstable, or of poor quality compared to specifications.

syntactical_description: Intermittently signals contact status due to auxiliary contact bounce
reasoning_trace: Intermittent signaling means the PLC or control system receives unreliable status. Erratic output.
end_effect: Product output is erratic, unstable, or of poor quality compared to specifications.

syntactical_description: Uncommanded signaling of contact status without main contact state change
reasoning_trace: False status signals can trigger incorrect control logic. Erratic system output.
end_effect: Product output is erratic, unstable, or of poor quality compared to specifications.

syntactical_description: Signals contact status too late relative to actual main contact position
reasoning_trace: Delayed signal causes timing mismatch in control sequences. Degraded performance.
end_effect: Primary function operates, but at a noticeably degraded speed, range, or capacity.

syntactical_description: Fails to cease signaling contact status when main contacts open
reasoning_trace: Stuck auxiliary contact signal misleads the control system about contactor state. Erratic output.
end_effect: Product output is erratic, unstable, or of poor quality compared to specifications.

syntactical_description: Erratically signals contact status with corrupted position indication
reasoning_trace: Corrupted status signals make contactor state unpredictable to the control system.
end_effect: Product output is erratic, unstable, or of poor quality compared to specifications.

syntactical_description: Fails to sense overload current through the bimetallic element
reasoning_trace: If the bimetallic element fails to sense overload, the motor has no thermal protection. Potential for motor winding overheating leading to fire or hazardous temperature exposure.
end_effect: Exposure of the user or environment to hazardous voltage, toxic materials, or extreme temperatures.

syntactical_description: Senses overload current with insufficient sensitivity due to degraded bimetallic calibration
reasoning_trace: Reduced sensitivity means the relay tolerates higher overload before tripping. Motor runs in degraded protection mode.
end_effect: Primary function operates, but at a noticeably degraded speed, range, or capacity.

syntactical_description: Intermittently senses overload current due to thermal drift
reasoning_trace: Intermittent sensing provides unreliable protection. Motor protection is erratic and unstable.
end_effect: Product output is erratic, unstable, or of poor quality compared to specifications.

syntactical_description: Uncommanded sense of overload current triggers nuisance trip at normal load
reasoning_trace: Nuisance trips shut down the motor unexpectedly. This is an abrupt cessation of primary function.
end_effect: Abrupt and unrecoverable cessation of the primary function during operation.

syntactical_description: Senses overload current too late allowing motor thermal damage
reasoning_trace: Delayed sensing allows the motor to overheat during prolonged overload. Potential thermal hazard.
end_effect: Exposure of the user or environment to hazardous voltage, toxic materials, or extreme temperatures.

syntactical_description: Fails to cease sense overload current after trip and reset
reasoning_trace: If sensing doesn't reset, the relay prevents motor restart. An inability to power on.
end_effect: Complete inability to power on, initialize, or accept user inputs.

syntactical_description: Erratically senses overload current with unstable trip threshold
reasoning_trace: Unpredictable trip thresholds make motor protection unreliable. Erratic output quality.
end_effect: Product output is erratic, unstable, or of poor quality compared to specifications.

syntactical_description: Fails to disconnect motor circuit during overload condition
reasoning_trace: If the trip mechanism fails, the motor is unprotected during overload. Motor windings can overheat catastrophically.
end_effect: Exposure of the user or environment to hazardous voltage, toxic materials, or extreme temperatures.

syntactical_description: Disconnects motor circuit with insufficient speed causing degraded protection
reasoning_trace: Slow disconnection allows excess energy to pass through. Motor operates but with degraded protection.
end_effect: Primary function operates, but at a noticeably degraded speed, range, or capacity.

syntactical_description: Intermittently disconnects motor circuit with unreliable trip actuation
reasoning_trace: Unreliable disconnection means protection is sometimes present and sometimes not. Erratic quality.
end_effect: Product output is erratic, unstable, or of poor quality compared to specifications.

syntactical_description: Uncommanded disconnecting of motor circuit without overload present
reasoning_trace: Nuisance trips shut down the motor abruptly during normal operation.
end_effect: Abrupt and unrecoverable cessation of the primary function during operation.

syntactical_description: Disconnects motor circuit too early before actual overload threshold is reached
reasoning_trace: Early disconnection limits motor capacity unnecessarily. Degraded operation.
end_effect: Primary function operates, but at a noticeably degraded speed, range, or capacity.

syntactical_description: Fails to cease disconnecting motor circuit preventing manual reset
reasoning_trace: If the mechanism latches permanently, the motor cannot be restarted. Inability to power on.
end_effect: Complete inability to power on, initialize, or accept user inputs.

syntactical_description: Erratically disconnects motor circuit with unpredictable trip behavior

end_effect: Product output is erratic, unstable, or of poor quality compared to specifications.

syntactical_description: Fails to signal trip status to the monitoring system

syntactical_description: Signals trip status too late after mechanical trip has occurred

syntactical_description: Erratically signals trip status with corrupted indication

syntactical_description: Fails to switch motor circuit between ON and OFF positions

end_effect: Complete inability to power on, initialize, or accept user inputs.

syntactical_description: Switches motor circuit with insufficient contact pressure causing degraded performance

end_effect: Primary function operates, but at a noticeably degraded speed, range, or capacity.
